title: Getting started with axR
text: axR talks Axivity AX3/AX6 accelerometer devices: discovery, status, settings, data download, – recent addition – reading recorded .cwa/AX6 binary files directly. wrapping Open Movement Project’s OMAPI C library (vendored src/omapi), rather reimplementing serial protocol binary file format scratch. OMAPI library behind Axivity’s OmGui software. ’s axivity_open()/close() step – OMAPI session starts automatically axR loaded, every device-facing function takes device_id get axivity_discover().

title: Getting started with axR
text: returns one row per connected device, device_id, serial, port, path, firmware_version, hardware_version, battery_level. Everything else axR talks live device takes device_id table.

title: Getting started with axR
text: handful read-queries – safe run time: change device state, harmless reversible: axivity_set_led() accepts "auto", "", "blue", "green", "cyan", "red", "magenta", "yellow", "white".
code: axivity_get_battery(id) axivity_self_test(id) axivity_get_memory_health(id) axivity_get_accelerometer(id)   # instantaneous x/y/z, in g axivity_get_time(id)            # device's RTC, as a POSIXct axivity_set_time(id, Sys.time())   # sync the device clock axivity_set_led(id, "blue")        # visible, easy way to confirm the                                     # write path is working

title: Getting started with axR
text: Delayed activation windows, session IDs, metadata, accelerometer configuration can read set: axivity_get_delays()/axivity_set_delays() use -Inf/Inf R-side sentinels OMAPI’s “always”/“never” delay values.
code: axivity_get_delays(id) axivity_get_session_id(id) axivity_get_metadata(id) axivity_get_accel_config(id)  axivity_set_accel_config(id, rate = 100, range = 8)  # 100 Hz, +/-8g

title: Getting started with axR
text: level one "none" (commit metadata – recommended, can cause data/metadata mismatch), "delete", "quickformat" (default), "wipe" (slowest, thorough). Staged settings changes (delays, session ID, metadata, accelerometer config) take full effect called – see OMAPI’s documentation detail . erases device. ’s undo.

title: Getting started with axR
text: long downloads, blocking = FALSE lets poll instead waiting:
code: info <- axivity_get_data_info(id) info  # size, filename, block layout, and the recorded time range  axivity_download(id, "session.cwa")  # blocks until done by default axivity_download(id, "session.cwa", blocking = FALSE) axivity_download_status(id)   # check progress without blocking axivity_download_wait(id)     # block until it finishes, whenever you're ready # axivity_download_cancel(id) # if you change your mind

what: If discovery isn’t finding your device
title: Getting started with axR
text: OMAPI’s device discovery device’s USB mass-storage mount two independent things – ’s possible storage side mount appear Finder/Explorer just fine OMAPI’s IOKit/SetupAPI device matching doesn’t find (happened axR’s development – see NEWS.md). happens, can see device’s volume directly: bypasses OMAPI/device_id entirely – ’s plain file copy whatever path give .
code: list.files("/Volumes")  # macOS -- find the device's volume name  axivity_copy_data("/Volumes/AX317_46171", "~/axr_data")

title: Getting started with axR
text: axivity_read_cwa() doesn’t need live device – works .cwa/AX6 file already disk, whether ’s one downloaded axivity_download()/axivity_copy_data(), file sitting directly device’s still-mounted volume (’s need copy first just read ): One row per sample: timestamp (POSIXct, sub-second precision), x/y/z (accelerometer, g), gx/gy/gz mx//mz (gyroscope/magnetometer, present recording – e.g. AX6 GA/GAM mode), light/temperature_c/battery_pct/ sample_rate (last four read per data block repeated across block’s samples, truly per-sample readings). device_id session_id handy quick sanity check ’ve opened file think . device_id independently cross-checked ioreg Axivity config web tool physical device. Known caveat: temperature_c currently trusted. vendored conversion formula specific one temperature sensor chip, ’s note beside source alternate formula different hardware – see ?axivity_read_cwa NEWS.md detail. Everything else output (timestamps, x/y/z, device_id) verified real device.

title: Getting started with axR
text: tibble axivity_read_cwa() returns meant clean starting point downstream wrist-actigraphy analysis zeitR – one row per sample, standard column names, POSIXct timestamps, accelerometer values already g rather device-raw units. (zeitR’s actual entry point may differ – check zeitR’s documentation current expected input shape.)

title: Getting started with axR
text: axR originally scoped “dumb pipe”: talk device, move bytes, leave file parsing downstream packages. axivity_read_cwa() deliberate exception – OMAPI already ships complete .cwa reader, wrapping directly avoids building second, differently-sourced parser format. axR still doesn’t higher-level actigraphy analysis (sleep detection, non-wear detection, etc.) data reads – ’s zeitR’s job, downstream tibble returns.

title: Getting started with axR
text: See NEWS.md full, current list, briefly: Device discovery real issues least one tested machine (macOS 26.2) despite device enumerating correctly USB/IOKit level – root cause still investigated. axivity_copy_data() workaround meantime. Windows device discovery uses fixed COM1-COM40 probe range rather true enumeration. temperature_c axivity_read_cwa() unverified likely wrong least hardware revisions. None tested AX6 (real AX3).

what: axR — Device communication and .cwa file reading for Axivity devices
title: axR — Device communication and .cwa file reading for Axivity devices
text: Device discovery, status, settings, data download Axivity AX3/AX6 accelerometer devices.

title: axR — Device communication and .cwa file reading for Axivity devices
text: axR talks Axivity AX3/AX6 accelerometers USB: discovering connected devices, querying setting status/configuration, downloading recorded .cwa files. Rather reimplementing Axivity serial protocol directly, axR wraps Open Movement Project’s OMAPI C library (vendored src/omapi, BSD 2-clause, Newcastle University — see src/omapi/LICENSE.TXT). OMAPI library behind Axivity’s OmGui software, includes maintained, platform-specific device discovery (IOKit/DiskArbitration macOS, SetupAPI Windows, udev Linux) rather hand-rolled equivalent. axR deliberately mostly “dumb pipe” — talks device moves bytes, leaving higher-level actigraphy analysis downstream packages mrpheus zeitR. axivity_read_cwa() deliberate exception: OMAPI already ships complete .cwa reader, axR wraps directly rather building second, differently-sourced parser. [!WARNING] axR fully verified real hardware. Live-device testing (macOS, real AX3) found fixed four separate crashes vendored library’s device-removal handling – see NEWS.md detail. timestamp/x/y/z/device_id axivity_read_cwa() verified correct real recording; temperature_c , likely wrong least hardware revisions. axivity_discover() currently finding real AX3 least one tested machine, despite device enumerating correctly OS level – axivity_copy_data()/axivity_read_cwa() work independently . Windows Linux device discovery compile clean untested real hardware either platform. tested AX6.

title: axR — Device communication and .cwa file reading for Axivity devices
text: 🔍 Discovery — axivity_discover() 📊 Status — battery, self-test, memory health, live accelerometer reading, RTC get/set, LED colour, anti-tamper lock, ECC flag, raw axivity_send_command() escape hatch ⚙️ Settings — delayed activation window, session ID, metadata scratch buffer, accelerometer rate/range, axivity_reset() (erase + commit, none/delete/quickformat/wipe levels) 📥 Download — axivity_download(), backed OMAPI’s background download thread (progress polling cancellation included), plain file copy 📂 axivity_copy_data() — fallback: plain file copy mounted device volume, bypassing OMAPI/device_id entirely, axivity_discover() isn’t finding device mass-storage mount still works 🧮 axivity_read_cwa() — parse .cwa/AX6 file directly (via OMAPI’s binary reader) tibble ready downstream actigraphy analysis — live device prior discovery required 🐞 axivity_enable_debug_log() — re-enable OMAPI’s internal diagnostic trace, debugging device-detection issues ’s axivity_open()/close() step — OMAPI session starts axR loaded stops ’s unloaded. Every device-facing function takes device_id axivity_discover().

title: axR — Device communication and .cwa file reading for Axivity devices
code: axR/ ├── configure              # generates src/Makevars at install time (chmod +x!) ├── cleanup                # removes configure/build artefacts (chmod +x!) ├── R/ │   ├── axR-package.R   # package-level documentation │   ├── zzz.R            # .onLoad/.onUnload (OmStartup/OmShutdown), .om_check() │   ├── discover.R        # axivity_discover(), axivity_enable_debug_log() │   ├── status.R          # battery, self-test, memory health, accelerometer, │   │                       # RTC, LED, lock, ECC, send_command │   ├── settings.R        # delays, session ID, metadata, accel config, reset │   ├── download.R        # data info, download, download_status/wait/cancel, │   │                       # axivity_copy_data() │   └── read_cwa.R        # axivity_read_cwa() ├── src/ │   ├── axR-omapi.cpp     # thin Rcpp wrapper around OMAPI │   ├── omapi/             # vendored OMAPI C library (BSD 2-clause), with │   │                       # axR patches -- see inline comments and NEWS.md │   ├── Makevars.in       # template; configure fills in the platform-specific bits │   └── Makevars.win      # Windows build config (static, no template needed) ├── vignettes/ │   ├── axR.Rmd.orig      # real vignette source (not shipped) │   └── axR.Rmd           # pre-computed output, knitr::knit()'d from .orig ├── tests/testthat/ ├── man/figures/ │   ├── logo.svg │   └── logo.png ├── pkgdown/favicon/      # via pkgdown::build_favicons() ├── .github/workflows/    # R-CMD-check.yaml, pkgdown.yaml ├── _pkgdown.yml ├── DESCRIPTION └── NEWS.md

what: axR: Device Communication and .cwa File Reading for Axivity Devices — axR-package
title: axR: Device Communication and .cwa File Reading for Axivity Devices — axR-package
text: Talks Axivity AX3/AX6 accelerometer devices: discovery, status (battery, self-test, memory health, accelerometer, RTC, LED, lock, ECC), settings (delays, session ID, metadata, accelerometer config, erase), data download, reading recorded .cwa/AX6 binary files (axivity_read_cwa()).

title: axR: Device Communication and .cwa File Reading for Axivity Devices — axR-package
text: axR originally scoped "dumb pipe" – talk device, move bytes, leave file parsing downstream packages. axivity_read_cwa() deliberate exception: OMAPI already ships complete binary file reader (omapi-reader.c), wrapping directly simpler consistent reimplementing format second time zeitR different reference pipeline. axR higher-level actigraphy analysis parsed data (sleep detection, non-wear detection, etc.) – still zeitR's job, downstream tibble returns.

title: axR: Device Communication and .cwa File Reading for Axivity Devices — axR-package
text: Rather reimplementing Axivity serial protocol .cwa binary format directly, axR wraps Open Movement Project's OMAPI C library (vendored src/omapi, BSD 2-clause, Newcastle University – see src/omapi/LICENSE.TXT). OMAPI library behind Axivity's OmGui software, includes maintained, platform-specific device discovery (IOKit/DiskArbitration macOS, SetupAPI Windows, udev Linux) rather hand-rolled equivalent. OMAPI session started axR loaded (OmStartup() .onLoad()) shut unloaded (OmShutdown() .onUnload()) – separate axivity_open()/close() step. Every device-facing function takes device_id, obtained axivity_discover(). axivity_read_cwa() axivity_copy_data() exceptions – work file already disk need live device connection .

title: axR: Device Communication and .cwa File Reading for Axivity Devices — axR-package
text: Maintainer: Lucas França lucas.franca@northumbria.ac.uk (ORCID) Authors: Lucas França lucas.franca@northumbria.ac.uk (ORCID) Mario Leocadio-Miguel mario.miguel@northumbria.ac.uk (ORCID)

what: Copy recorded data directly off a mounted Axivity volume — axivity_copy_data
title: Copy recorded data directly off a mounted Axivity volume — axivity_copy_data
text: fallback axivity_discover()/OMAPI device access working, device's USB mass-storage volume mounts visible Finder/diskutil regardless – common split, since storage side OMAPI's IOKit-level device discovery independent paths (see NEWS.md discovery issues hit far). bypasses OMAPI device_id entirely: plain file copy, nothing . axR parse .cwa contents – see mrpheus zeitR .

title: Copy recorded data directly off a mounted Axivity volume — axivity_copy_data
code: axivity_copy_data(   device_path,   dest_dir,   pattern = "\\.cwa$",   overwrite = FALSE )

title: Copy recorded data directly off a mounted Axivity volume — axivity_copy_data
text: device_path Character. Path mounted device volume, e.g. "/Volumes/CWA17_46171". Find Finder, list.files("/Volumes"). dest_dir Character. Destination directory downloaded files. Created (recursively) already exist. pattern Character. Regex filter files copied, matched case-insensitively. Default "\\.cwa$". overwrite Logical. Overwrite existing files destination. Default FALSE.

title: Copy recorded data directly off a mounted Axivity volume — axivity_copy_data
text: Character vector destination file paths successfully copied.

title: Discover connected Axivity devices — axivity_discover
text: Wraps OMAPI's OmGetDeviceIds() plus handful per-device status calls single data frame. Unlike serial-port probing, uses OMAPI's device discovery – including platform-specific finder (IOKit/DiskArbitration macOS, SetupAPI Windows, udev Linux) – behave way OmGui machine.

title: Discover connected Axivity devices — axivity_discover
Discover connected Axivity devices — axivity_discover
text: data frame one row per connected device: device_id, serial, port, path, firmware_version, hardware_version, battery_level. Zero rows devices connected.

what: Download recorded data off an Axivity device — axivity_download
title: Download recorded data off an Axivity device — axivity_download
text: Wraps OMAPI's OmBeginDownloading(), runs download background thread inside library. axR parse .cwa contents – see mrpheus zeitR .

title: Download recorded data off an Axivity device — axivity_download
code: axivity_download(   device_id,   dest_file,   offset_blocks = 0L,   length_blocks = -1L,   blocking = TRUE )

title: Download recorded data off an Axivity device — axivity_download
text: device_id Integer device ID, returned axivity_discover(). dest_file Character. Destination file path (existing files path truncated). offset_blocks Integer. Start offset download, blocks. Default 0. length_blocks Integer. Length download, blocks. Default -1 (). blocking Logical. TRUE (default), block download completes, fails, cancelled – equivalent calling axivity_download_wait() immediately . FALSE, return soon download starts; poll axivity_download_status().

title: Download recorded data off an Axivity device — axivity_download
text: blocking = TRUE, list status (one "complete", "error", "cancelled") value (diagnostic code status "error"). blocking = FALSE, invisibly NULL.

Check or wait for an Axivity device's download progress — axivity_download_status

what: Enable OMAPI's internal debug log — axivity_enable_debug_log
title: Enable OMAPI's internal debug log — axivity_enable_debug_log
text: diagnostic escape hatch axivity_discover() finding device expect . OMAPI logs internally via OmLog(), axR's default log target NULL (compiled code write stderr unprompted – see NEWS.md). re-enables .

title: Enable OMAPI's internal debug log — axivity_enable_debug_log
Enable OMAPI's internal debug log — axivity_enable_debug_log
text: file Character path log file, NULL (default) log stderr. file reliable diagnostic purposes: OMAPI's discovery thread logs background pthread, raw stderr writes non-R thread always reach R console/terminal depending frontend – file sidesteps ambiguity.

title: Enable OMAPI's internal debug log — axivity_enable_debug_log
text: Invisibly, OMAPI status code (negative indicates failure, e.g. file opened).

title: Enable OMAPI's internal debug log — axivity_enable_debug_log
text: Important: controls log lines go. Whether anything logged controlled OMAPI's debug level, read OMDEBUG environment variable , OmStartup() time – .e. library(axR) runs. seeing log output calling , set OMDEBUG (e.g. Sys.setenv(OMDEBUG = "3")) loading axR, fresh R session, call function retry.

what: Read a .cwa/AX6 binary file into a tibble — axivity_read_cwa
title: Read a .cwa/AX6 binary file into a tibble — axivity_read_cwa
text: Uses OMAPI's binary file reader (omapi-reader.c, vendored libomapi) parse .cwa/AX6 recording directly, rather reimplementing binary format scratch. Returns one row per sample – ready hand zeitR, downstream actigraphy analysis.

title: Read a .cwa/AX6 binary file into a tibble — axivity_read_cwa
Read a .cwa/AX6 binary file into a tibble — axivity_read_cwa
text: path Character. Path .cwa/AX6 file.

title: Read a .cwa/AX6 binary file into a tibble — axivity_read_cwa
text: tibble (plain data frame, tibble package installed) one row per sample: timestamp POSIXct, UTC, sub-second precision x, y, z Accelerometer readings, g gx, gy, gz Gyroscope readings, raw units (present recording gyroscope, e.g. AX6 GA/GAM mode) mx, , mz Magnetometer readings, raw units (present recording magnetometer, e.g. AX6 GAM mode) light Raw light sensor reading temperature_c Temperature degrees Celsius. Unverified, possibly wrong – OMAPI's conversion (OM_VALUE_TEMPERATURE_MC) hardcodes formula one specific temperature sensor chip (MCP9700); comment beside vendored source notes alternate formula different chip (MCP9701), suggesting may hardware/revision-specific. Cross-check OmGui's reading file relying . battery_pct Battery percentage, time block sample_rate Sampling rate Hz, time block device_id, session_id, metadata attached attributes. timestamp, x/y/z, device_id verified correct real AX3 file (cross-checked device_id ioreg Axivity config web tool); temperature_c .

title: Read a .cwa/AX6 binary file into a tibble — axivity_read_cwa
text: Unlike rest axR, function talk live device – works file already disk (e.g. one retrieved axivity_copy_data() axivity_download()), require axivity_discover() found anything.

what: Erase an Axivity device's data storage and commit settings — axivity_reset
title: Erase an Axivity device's data storage and commit settings — axivity_reset
text: Wraps OMAPI's OmEraseDataAndCommit(). Staged settings changes (delays, session ID, metadata, accelerometer config) take full effect called.

title: Erase an Axivity device's data storage and commit settings — axivity_reset
Erase an Axivity device's data storage and commit settings — axivity_reset
text: device_id Integer device ID, returned axivity_discover(). level One "none" (commit metadata , recommended – can cause data/metadata mismatch), "delete" (remove recreate data file), "quickformat" (recreate filesystem), "wipe" (clear NAND blocks, cleanest slowest).

Run an Axivity device's built-in self-test — axivity_self_test
text: device_id Integer device ID, returned axivity_discover().

title: Run an Axivity device's built-in self-test — axivity_self_test
text: list passed (logical) diagnostic_code (opaque, firmware-specific code; 0 means passed).

what: Send a raw command to an Axivity device — axivity_send_command
title: Send a raw command to an Axivity device — axivity_send_command
text: escape hatch wrapping OMAPI's OmCommand(), anything covered axR's typed functions. generally recommended – OMAPI's docs note incorrect use lead unspecified behaviour.

title: Send a raw command to an Axivity device — axivity_send_command
Send a raw command to an Axivity device — axivity_send_command
text: device_id Integer device ID, returned axivity_discover(). command Character. command string send. expected Character. expected response prefix, "" specified. timeout_ms Integer. Timeout milliseconds. Default 2000.

title: Send a raw command to an Axivity device — axivity_send_command

title: Set an Axivity device's LED colour — axivity_set_led
Set an Axivity device's LED colour — axivity_set_led
text: device_id Integer device ID, returned axivity_discover(). colour One "auto" (default device-controlled behaviour), "", "blue", "green", "cyan", "red", "magenta", "yellow", "white".

title: axR 0.1.0 (2026-07)
text: Initial release. Talks Axivity AX3/AX6 accelerometer devices: discovery, status, settings, data download, reading recorded .cwa/AX6 binary files – wrapping Open Movement Project’s OMAPI C library (vendored src/omapi, BSD 2-clause, Newcastle University), rather reimplementing serial protocol binary file format directly. OMAPI library behind Axivity’s OmGui software. axivity_discover() – backed OmGetDeviceIds() plus per-device status calls, returning device_id, serial, port, path, firmware_version, hardware_version, battery_level. Every device-facing function takes device_id table – ’s axivity_open()/close() step; OMAPI session starts axR loaded (OmStartup() .onLoad()) stops ’s unloaded. Status: axivity_get_battery(), axivity_self_test(), axivity_get_memory_health(), axivity_get_accelerometer(), axivity_get_time()/set_time() (POSIXct), axivity_set_led(), axivity_is_locked()/set_lock()/unlock(), axivity_get_ecc()/ set_ecc(), axivity_send_command() raw escape hatch (OmCommand()). Settings: axivity_get_delays()/set_delays() (-Inf/Inf R-side sentinels OMAPI’s zero/infinite OM_DATETIME values), axivity_get_session_id()/set_session_id(), axivity_get_metadata()/set_metadata(), axivity_get_accel_config()/set_accel_config(), axivity_reset(level = c("none","delete","quickformat","wipe")) matching OMAPI’s OM_ERASE_LEVEL enum directly. Data download: axivity_get_data_info() (size, filename, block layout, recorded time range), axivity_download() wrapping OmBeginDownloading() (OMAPI’s background-thread download) axivity_download_status()/_wait()/_cancel() polling cancellation. axivity_copy_data(device_path, dest_dir, ...) – fallback bypasses OMAPI/device_id entirely copies .cwa files straight mounted volume path. use axivity_discover() isn’t finding device via OMAPI’s discovery, mass-storage side still mounts fine regardless (see Known limitations). axivity_read_cwa(path) – parses .cwa/AX6 file directly via OMAPI’s binary reader (omapi-reader.c, already vendored), rather leaving parsing zeitR building second, differently-sourced parser. deliberate exception axR’s “dumb pipe” scope – see ?axivity_read_cwa. Whole block-reading loop runs C++, R (multi-day 100Hz recording millions samples). Returns tibble (plain data frame tibble isn’t installed): one row per sample, timestamp (POSIXct, sub-second precision), x/y/z (accelerometer, g), gx/gy/gz mx//mz (gyro/magnetometer, present – e.g. AX6 GA/GAM mode), light/temperature_c/battery_pct/sample_rate block granularity. device_id/session_id/metadata attached attributes. Doesn’t require live device prior discovery – works .cwa/AX6 file already disk, including one still sitting mounted device volume. axivity_enable_debug_log(file = NULL) – re-enables OMAPI’s internal OmLog() diagnostic trace (stderr default, file, since raw stderr writes OMAPI’s background discovery pthread don’t always reach R console depending frontend). Debug level separately controlled OMDEBUG environment variable, read OmStartup() time – .e. library(axR), fresh session.

what: 🐛 Bug fixes – vendored OMAPI, found via real-device and CI testing
title: axR 0.1.0 (2026-07)
text: Four separate crashes found fixed via live-device testing macOS (26.2, real AX3), plus round R-CMD-check actually compiling Linux Windows device finders first time (previously ever built/tested macOS): CFRelease(deviceData->deviceName) crashed *** CFRelease() called NULL *** (EXC_BREAKPOINT) unplug. Older CoreFoundation treated CFRelease(NULL) silent -op; recent macOS hardens hard abort – decade-old latent bug vendored code, newly fatal rather newly introduced. Fixed NULL guard. Release() deviceData->deviceInterface crashed SIGSEGV even deviceInterface non-NULL – vtable pointed longer valid time fires (physical device already gone). Stopped calling . double-free (free(deviceData) twice, SIGABRT/___BUG_IN_CLIENT_OF_LIBMALLOC_...) consistent IOKit delivering one removal notification single physical unplug current macOS. Fixed idempotency guard (DeviceData.removed) – first attempt guard flawed (stored flag inside memory function also free()d, duplicate call’s guard check use--free still let double-free ). Fixed properly longer freeing deviceData handler – bounded, negligible per-removal-event leak exchange guard actually reliable. Also removed custom SIGINT handler called exit(0) directly, plus several fprintf(stderr, ...) calls duplicating adjacent OmLog() calls. vendored library installing process-wide signal handler terminates process actively unsafe embedded host application (R) interrupt handling. kIOMasterPortDefault -> kIOMainPortDefault (macOS 12+ deprecation warning; pure rename, behaviour change). Three exit(1) calls udev_new() failure terminate whole R process. Replaced early returns; callers already handle failure gracefully. file never used OmLog() , raw printf()/ fprintf(stderr, ...), including one unconditional debug printf() inside background udev monitoring loop spam stdout every event. Converted OmLog() (matching macOS finder’s convention) Linux keeps diagnostic capability via axivity_enable_debug_log(). Untested real Linux hardware – correctness fixes R CMD check flagged, claim discovery works end--end . GUID_DEVINTERFACE_DISK/GUID_DEVINTERFACE_VOLUME undefined link time. First attempt (#define INITGUID) made things worse – caused winioctl.h processed twice (directly, transitively via windows.h) INITGUID active times, producing "redefinition const GUID ..." errors ~20 GUIDs. Reverted; fixed instead -luuid Makevars.win, standard MinGW-w64 fix exact symptom. VariantClear/SysFreeString (-loleaut32) CLSID_WbemLocator/IID_IWbemLocator (-lwbemuuid) undefined: added PKG_LIBS. _com_util::ConvertStringToBSTR (via _bstr_t/bstr_t("...") two WMI queries) undefined – confirmed via "find -lcomsuppw: file directory" Rtools45’s MinGW-w64 doesn’t ship MSVC-specific library backing . live call sites (bstr_t matches inside /* ... */ dead-code block, never compiled) rewritten use plain SysAllocString()/SysFreeString() instead. Deprecated ftime() (glibc: “Use gettimeofday clock_gettime instead”) omapi-internal.c omapi-devicefinder-linux.c, flagged significant install-time warning Linux CI runner. Switched clock_gettime(CLOCK_REALTIME, ...). sprintf() -> snprintf() throughout omapi-status.c, omapi-settings.c, omapi-devicefinder-mac.c (bounded, behaviour change). OmStartup()’s default log stream changed stderr NULL (opt-, via OmSetLogStream()/ axivity_enable_debug_log()). vendored-code divergences upstream libomapi flagged inline // axR patch comments, anyone re-vendoring later.

title: axR 0.1.0 (2026-07)
text: .github/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml: ubuntu/macOS/windows matrix, matching zeitR/mrpheus, plus two axR-specific steps: installing libudev-dev Linux (auto-detected prose SystemRequirements field) chmod +x configure cleanup (POSIX ). .github/workflows/pkgdown.yaml: build/coverage-badge/deploy-- gh-pages structure matching zeitR/mrpheus (via JamesIves/github-pages-deploy-action), extra steps . _pkgdown.yml: Bootstrap 5 + bslib theming matching zeitR/mrpheus’s structure, using axR’s hex-sticker palette (navy #014370, coral #FC544A, peach #FFA75D, cream #FFECD4). configure/cleanup (POSIX shell scripts, need chmod +x): src/Makevars generated src/Makevars.install time rather committed statically, moving Darwin/Linux conditional Make syntax (ifeq, $(shell), :=) shell – fixes R CMD check “GNU extensions Makefiles” warning. cleanup removes generated/compiled artefacts fresh check. man/figures/logo.png (rasterized via rsvg-convert) pkgdown/favicon/ (via pkgdown::build_favicons()) added. README: R CMD CHECK / coverage / pkgdown-site badges; logo moved inline H1 (standalone lower file rendered huge unconstrained pkgdown home page). Netlify deployment (watching gh-pages, axr.circadia-lab.uk) manual step outside repo.

title: axR 0.1.0 (2026-07)
text: vignette("axR") – discovery, status/settings, downloading, axivity_copy_data(), axivity_read_cwa(). Uses pre-computed vignette pattern: vignettes/axR.Rmd.orig real source (excluded built package); vignettes/axR.Rmd generated via knitr::knit(), run locally, real output baked – r-universe/CRAN never need execute axR code build . axivity_read_cwa() section eval = TRUE far, since ’s part verified real hardware; re-run re-bake package gets verified.

title: axR 0.1.0 (2026-07)
text: .om_check() behaviour (list scalar status, pass-vs. error); axivity_discover() shape check device connected; axivity_reset()/axivity_set_led() argument validation (match.arg() failures) – none require hardware.

title: axR 0.1.0 (2026-07)
text: Mario Leocadio-Miguel added author.

title: axR 0.1.0 (2026-07)
text: axivity_discover() yet finding real AX3 device least one tested machine (macOS 26.2), despite ioreg confirming device enumerates correctly IOKit level expected VID/PID (0x04D8/0x0057) serial. Root cause yet identified – waiting input Axivity/OMAPI contact. axivity_copy_data()/axivity_read_cwa() work independently meantime, since device’s mass-storage mount isn’t affected. axivity_read_cwa()’s temperature_c unverified likely wrong least hardware revisions – vendored conversion formula specific one temperature sensor chip, different formula noted another chip right beside source. timestamp, x/y/z, device_id verified correct real AX3 recording. Windows Linux device discovery compile clean untested real hardware either platform. Windows discovery uses fixed COM1-COM40 probe range rather true device enumeration. tested AX6, real AX3. axivity_get_metadata()’s padding-trim regex hasn’t checked real device’s returned buffer. card.png (pkgdown social-preview card) yet – hex sticker logo .
